hyp.name: H3-RetargetMix: instruction-consistent action relabeling via cross-demo eef-frame transplant (egocentric action recompute)
hyp.mechanism: Break the (scene→canonical-motor) shortcut at the ACTION TARGET, not the representation. For each training frame t in demo A (object O at canonical position p_A, with the free grasp_target g_A = eef-xyz at tightest grip already computed in dataset.py), mine a second demo B for the SAME object O (same language_instruction string) whose grasp_target g_B is far from g_A (>= delta meters; libero-object objects do drift across demos, and we additionally pull cross-suite demos that share the object noun). Build a composite training example: KEEP demo A's current observation image/proprio, but RELABEL the action chunk so it points toward g_B instead of g_A. Concretely we do NOT re-render: we take demo B's action chunk and re-express it in demo A's current eef frame via a rigid SE(2/3) retarget: a_new = R*(a_B) computed so that integrating a_new from A's current eef-xyz lands on g_B with B's approach/grasp gripper schedule. Because proprio (eef pose) and the 7-dim delta actions are all real and present in RLDS, this is a closed-form action relabel, no simulator. Now the SAME visual scene appears paired with DIFFERENT action targets depending on which instruction/target was selected — but here the instruction string is identical (same object), so to make the target predictable the policy MUST read the object's CURRENT visible location, killing position-memorization. Train: action L1 on the mix of (original A→g_A) and (retargeted A→g_B) examples, 50/50, warm-start 2500 steps. Supervision = proprio + actions only (real-transferable).
hyp.why_swap: H1/H2a-c left the action TARGET unchanged, so position stayed a sufficient statistic and L1 kept decoding it. This is the first H that makes two different action targets share an input distribution, so minimizing L1 is IMPOSSIBLE by reading position alone — the loss landscape itself now rewards localizing the object. It directly manufactures the position-invariance the swap eval needs, using only real frames+proprio.
hyp.novelty_guess: Closed-form egocentric ACTION-RELABEL transplant across demos to decorrelate action-target from scene position is distinct from ReconVLA (reconstruction), ObjectVLA (VL pairs), RoboPoint (sim-GT keypoints), and LIBERO-Plus (new trajectories). Hindsight-relabeling exists in RL but not as a position-shortcut-breaker for VLA grounding with proprio-derived targets.

verdict.reason:
This is genuinely DIFFERENT from H1/H2a-c and is the first hypothesis that attacks the right object: the ACTION TARGET, not the representation. The diagnosis was that grounding never reaches the action because position stays a sufficient statistic for L1. H3 directly destroys that statistic by making the SAME visual scene map to two different action targets — that IS the mechanism that can move swap off 0.00, and it is data-free (reuses RLDS frames/proprio/actions), real-transferable (supervision = eef proprio + delta actions, both exist on real robots), and warm-start-feasible. I confirmed the model uses an L1RegressionActionHead (finetune_fused.py: compute_actions_l1_loss), so the hypothesis's 'L1 on action' premise is CORRECT, not a token mismatch. grasp_target is already computed as raw eef xyz at tightest grip (rlds/dataset.py:179-181), free and real. So it clears every hard constraint and is not pre-empted by the named prior art (ReconVLA/ObjectVLA/RoboPoint/LIBERO-Plus). NOT a kill.

BUT three serious risks the proposal understates: (1) The 'closed-form rigid SE(2/3) retarget so integrating a_new lands on g_B' is NOT valid as stated. LIBERO actions are DELTA-EEF commands (transforms.py libero_dataset_transform) fed to an OSC_POSE controller whose realized motion depends on contact dynamics and closed-loop correction. You cannot open-loop integrate R*a_B from A's current eef and land precisely at g_B; the relabeled chunk is a kinematically inconsistent target the env could never have produced, so the policy may learn to emit unexecutable deltas that fail at rollout even if L1 drops in train. A defensible fix: don't transplant B's chunk; instead synthesize a fresh straight-line-to-g_B delta chunk in A's eef frame (reach-only, copy B's gripper schedule) — but that only covers the approach phase, not grasp/contact. (2) Logical tension in 'same instruction string': the proposal says keep the instruction identical so the policy MUST read current visible position — but then BOTH the original example (A->g_A) and the relabeled one (A->g_B) share IDENTICAL image AND identical instruction yet demand different targets. That is an inconsistent label: the input is literally the same, so L1 is minimized by predicting the AVERAGE of g_A and g_B, which grounds to nothing. The decorrelation only works if the input differs — i.e., the target must be inferable from something visible. The named object IS at g_A in A's image, NOT at g_B, so the correct, learnable signal is to read g_A; relabeling A's frame to g_B teaches the policy to ignore the image, the opposite of the goal. This is a real conceptual bug: hindsight-relabeling requires the goal to match the OBSERVATION, which a cross-demo transplant violates. (3) 'libero-object objects drift across demos' — within one task the target object position IS randomized per demo, so same-object/different-position pairs exist, but those pairs also have DIFFERENT images, which is exactly the natural training signal already present; the swap collapse persists despite that, so simply mixing more same-object/different-position real frames (no relabel) is the cheaper ablation and likely the actual active ingredient. SHARPENED: drop the cross-demo action transplant; instead do OBSERVATION-CONSISTENT goal-conditioning — relabel A's target to A's own visible object location (g_A) but make the policy predict the delta to that location FROM the image as an explicit goal token, and add hard negatives where the image's object is moved (use the existing different-position same-object demos B with their OWN images and OWN targets g_B). That keeps target inferable from observation, is still data-free/real-transferable, and is the honest version of 'two scenes, two targets.' As written, swap_likelihood is MEDIUM at best and the retarget math + label-inconsistency could make it NULL like the others; worth running only after fixing the observation-target consistency, and the first run should be the cheap no-relabel same-object-position-mixing ablation to confirm the premise.
verdict.sharpened: Cross-demo action transplant onto A's frame creates an observation-inconsistent label (same image+instruction -> two targets => L1 averages, policy ignores image); fix by goal-conditioning on the object's OWN visible location with different-position same-object demos kept WITH their own images. Also the SE(3) 'lands at g_B' integration is invalid for delta-EEF/OSC contact dynamics.

hyp.name: H3-TTVP: Test-time visual prompting — paint the named object's region into the input frame (detector-grounded), with a tiny region-token adapter
hyp.mechanism: At INFERENCE, before `prepare_images_for_vla`, run an open-vocabulary detector (OWLv2 / GroundingDINO / YOLO-World — all real-RGB, real-transferable) on `obs['full_image']` with the parsed named object string from the bddl `(:language)` block. Take the top box for the named object and DRAW IT INTO THE PIXELS the policy actually consumes: a high-contrast colored bounding ellipse/box + a slight desaturation (alpha~0.35) of everything outside the box (a 'spotlight' on the target). This modified RGB is what flows through DINO-SigLIP into the projector and into the LLM self-attention over patches. Because the action model is demonstrably a Vision->Action model that reads patch tokens, moving the *salient visual evidence of the target* to the swapped location physically relocates the patches the action attends to. Two regimes: (a) ZERO-TRAIN — just prompt the released checkpoint and measure; (b) a ~2500-step warm-start where we render the SAME spotlight overlay on training frames using grasp-proprio-derived target pixels (project the demo's eef-xyz-at-grasp into the camera via the known LIBERO extrinsics/intrinsics, OR simply place the spotlight at the detector's box on training RGB) so the policy learns to FOLLOW the spotlight rather than treat it as a corruption. Supervision for warm-start = existing demo actions (unchanged); the overlay location at train time = detector box on the same RGB (real-transferable) — no sim-only signal. Why it differs from H2a-c: those fed a *localized vector* into a representation the action ignored; this changes the *raw visual input distribution* at the named location, which is the exact channel the position-memorizing policy is known to use.
hyp.why_swap: Swap stayed 0.00 because the policy localizes by reading patch tokens at the MEMORIZED pixel location and the named object moved there but the policy never looks at the new location. Painting a spotlight at the detector-found new location forces the high-saliency target evidence to appear at the swapped pixels, so the patch tokens the action attends to now carry the object at its true (swapped) position. This intervenes on the actual causal variable (input pixels at the target location) instead of an auxiliary head the action discards.
hyp.novelty_guess: Test-time visual prompting (Red-Circle / FGVP / fine-grained visual prompting) is established for VLMs/CLIP, but applying a detector-grounded saliency SPOTLIGHT to the input frame of a small action-regression VLA to defeat positional-memorization on LIBERO-PRO swap — and warm-starting the policy to FOLLOW the spotlight — appears novel for VLA grounding (distinct from ReconVLA's reconstruction aux and RoboPoint's sim-keypoint planner).

verdict.reason:
NOT a re-skin of H1/H2a-c. Those failed because they injected a localized VECTOR into an internal representation the action regressor demonstrably discards. TTVP intervenes on the exact causal variable the diagnosis names: the RAW INPUT PIXELS / patch tokens the Vision->Action policy actually reads. Confirmed against the code: prepare_images_for_vla (openvla_utils.py:714) is a clean chokepoint where the agentview RGB is the literal input to DINO-SigLIP; the named object string is recoverable at inference from read_bddl_language + _parse_obj_of_interest (real-RGB OWLv2/GroundingDINO query, real-transferable); position-swap only relocates the object via BDDL (On obj region) with instruction unchanged, so a detector-grounded spotlight at the swapped pixels does move the salient evidence to where the object now is. This is a genuinely different lever and it has a real chance of moving swap off 0.00, hence not kill.

But three hard risks keep it at MEDIUM, not high:
(1) ZERO-TRAIN regime (a) will very likely FAIL and may even crash clean accuracy. A spotlight/desaturation overlay is OOD corruption for a checkpoint that never saw it; StableVLA's whole selling point is an info-bottleneck that FILTERS visual noise — it may actively suppress the painted overlay as a corruption. Report (a) only as an ablation; do not expect it to work alone.
(2) num_images_in_input=2: a SECOND wrist image flows in as separate pixel_values (confirmed: obs['wrist_image'], cfg.num_images_in_input>1 branch in get_vla_action). The memorization shortcut may live partly in the agentview spatial prior; if you only paint the agentview, the policy can still ignore the spotlight, and if the wrist view is uninformative the spotlight competes with a strong agentview position prior. Must decide whether/how to overlay the wrist view (the eef-xyz->pixel projection differs per camera).
(3) The warm-start regime (b) is the part that actually matters, and it quietly re-introduces the H2 failure mode unless designed carefully: if the spotlight at train time is placed at the DETECTOR box on un-swapped training RGB, the detector box and the memorized location COINCIDE (training data is never swapped — LIBERO-PRO is eval-only). So the policy can learn 'grasp at memorized location' while treating the spotlight as redundant/ignorable — exactly H2a's 'learns the aux signal, action ignores it.' The spotlight only becomes load-bearing if training breaks the spotlight<->memorized-location correlation (e.g. randomly jitter/move the spotlight to a non-grasp location on a fraction of frames and supervise the action to follow it, or train on multi-object scenes where the spotlight disambiguates which of several candidate locations to grasp). Without that decorrelation, warm-start is predicted NULL for the same reason H2 was.

Detector reliability and pixel mismatch are secondary risks (OWLv2 on 256px agentview, small/occluded LIBERO objects, top-box errors) but tolerable.

Novelty: detector-grounded saliency SPOTLIGHT painted into the input frame of a small action-regression VLA + warm-starting the policy to FOLLOW it is distinct from the listed prior art (ReconVLA=reconstruction aux; RoboPoint=sim-keypoint planner; ObjectVLA=VL identity pairs; LIBERO-Plus=mix-SFT). Red-Circle/FGVP visual prompting exists for CLIP/VLMs but not, to my knowledge, as a position-memorization fix for VLA action heads. Mild overlap with general visual-prompting -> 'novel' but flag the lineage.

Real-transferable: YES — OWLv2/GroundingDINO run on real RGB, named object from instruction parse, demo actions unchanged. The optional eef-xyz->pixel projection uses LIBERO extrinsics (sim) but is only a convenience for placing the train-time overlay; the detector-box alternative is fully real-transferable, so the METHOD's foundation is not sim-only.

Sharpened claim / decisive test: run two quick checks before committing the full warm-start. (i) ZERO-TRAIN swap eval with spotlight — if SR stays 0.00 AND clean drops, the policy treats the overlay as corruption -> warm-start is mandatory. (ii) The make-or-break warm-start ablation: spotlight placed at detector box (correlated with grasp) vs spotlight deliberately MOVED to a decoy location with action supervised to follow it. If only the correlated version trains and swap stays 0.00, TTVP has collapsed into H2 and should be killed; if the decorrelated version makes the action track the spotlight, swap should move. Design the warm-start to decorrelate spotlight from memorized location, or this becomes H2a in pixel clothing.
verdict.sharpened: Pixel-space spotlight is the right causal lever (unlike H2's discarded vector), but warm-start MUST decorrelate the spotlight from the memorized grasp location (jitter/decoy + follow-supervision), else it collapses back into H2a 'learns aux, action ignores'; zero-train regime likely fails as OOD corruption the IB filters out.

hyp.name: H3c — Detector-distilled HARD NEGATIVE position swaps in training (synthesize swap at the LABEL level using existing frames + detector boxes; no re-rendering)
hyp.mechanism: Use the detector (offline, existing frames) to get boxes for ALL candidate objects in each scene, not just the target. Then, DATA-FREE and WITHOUT re-rendering, construct training-time hard negatives at the SUPERVISION level: for a fraction p of batches, keep the SAME image but pair it with a DIFFERENT instruction naming a different detected object in the scene, and set the action label to the target-relative reach toward THAT object's detector anchor (computable because we know each object's a* from its box, and the relative-reach geometry transfers; only the reach/approach segment is relabeled, manipulation-specific fine motion is masked out of the loss for synthetic pairs). This is the inverse of H2b's counterfactual: H2b pushed the LOCALIZER prediction off-target but left the ACTION label fixed; here the ACTION LABEL itself is rewritten to reach the renamed object's detected location. The model sees (same pixels, instruction A -> reach object A's box) and (same pixels, instruction B -> reach object B's box), so identical visual input with different language MUST produce different reach actions — directly destroying the vision-only positional shortcut.
hyp.why_swap: Every prior failure left a one-to-one image->action mapping intact, so the model never had a training example where the SAME image demanded a DIFFERENT action based on language — it had no incentive to read the instruction for positioning. This injects exactly that contradiction using only existing frames + detector boxes (real-transferable: detector boxes exist on real RGB; relative-reach geometry is task-agnostic). At swap eval, the model has been explicitly trained that 'which object I reach depends on the instruction + that object's current detected location,' which is the swap competency. Confidence/agreement filtering (only synthesize pairs where the detector firmly localizes both objects) tolerates noisy sim labels. Moves swap off 0.00 by making instruction-conditioned reach a TRAINING REQUIREMENT, not an emergent hope.
hyp.novelty_guess: Relabeling the action target (not re-rendering pixels) using multi-object detector boxes to manufacture instruction->position hard negatives is distinct from LIBERO-Plus's 20k NEW trajectories (real new data, 7B) and from sim counterfactual re-rendering (forbidden). It is a label-space, data-free, detector-distilled hard-negative scheme; I have not seen this exact construction for VLA positional debiasing.

verdict.reason: Mechanism is genuinely DIFFERENT from the failed H1/H2a-c, not a re-skin. Verified in code: H2a-c grounded the representation (aux head prismatic/util/grasp_head.py, in-model TargetLocalizer prismatic/util/target_localizer.py) and hoped it reached the action — all NULL because the image->action map stayed one-to-one. H3c instead rewrites the ACTION LABEL so (same pixels, instruction A) and (same pixels, instruction B) demand DIFFERENT action token sequences. This is the one thing every prior failure lacked: an explicit training-time contradiction that makes the vision-only positional shortcut INCUR LOSS. It directly attacks the documented 'language ignorance' root cause, so it is not a representation-only tweak -> not an auto-kill. Real-transferable: detector boxes exist on real RGB. BUT the load-bearing 'free label' claim is false as written, which is why feasible_data_free_warmstart=false. The action space (prismatic/vla/constants.py LIBERO_CONSTANTS) is an 8-step chunk of 7-DoF DELTA eef commands, tokenized as discrete strings — NOT a 3D point or a 'reach-toward-box' vector. To relabel toward object B the method must (1) get a 3D target for B, but the detector only yields a 2D box and the proprio-derived grasp_target (dataset.py:177-181) exists ONLY for the real target the robot actually reached, never for B — so 'each object's a* from its box' needs an un-projection (depth/calibration) the repo doesn't have; (2) synthesize a kinematically valid delta-action trajectory from current eef pose to B, i.e. a scripted reach/IK motion-planner, then train BC on actions the demonstrator never executed (off-manifold: wrong approach angle/orientation deltas, no collision avoidance). 'Masking fine motion' still leaves the coarse reach deltas — exactly the fabricated part. So it is data-free in PIXELS but NOT in labels: the supervision is a synthesized motion rollout, not a free signal. Swap_likelihood medium not high: if a decent scripted reach is built, the contradiction could genuinely lift swap off 0.00 (true novel mechanism); but off-distribution synthetic actions risk degrading clean and the model may learn a degenerate 'reach the box centroid' policy rather than real grasping. Novelty: label-space detector-distilled hard-negative reach-relabeling is distinct from LIBERO-Plus (20k NEW real trajectories, 7B), sim counterfactual re-rendering (forbidden), and ReconVLA/ObjectVLA/RoboPoint. Recommendation: do NOT kill, but re-scope effort M->L and demand the reach-synthesizer + 2D->3D step be specified before running; the 'free label' framing hides a mini motion planner that determines whether this works.
verdict.sharpened: The contradiction (same pixels, different instruction -> different action label) is the right and novel fix, but the 'free' label is actually a SYNTHESIZED delta-action reach trajectory requiring 2D-box->3D un-projection + a scripted IK reach — feasibility and clean-accuracy hinge entirely on that un-stated motion-synthesizer, so it is data-free in pixels but not in labels.

synthesis:
I now have complete mechanistic understanding. The decisive insight confirmed by the code:

**The action head fuses `proprio` (current eef pose, fed directly as `p`) with `h_t` (task/vision tokens) and `h_a` (action hidden states).** The L1 target is the normalized delta-action chunk. Given the start eef pose (proprio) and a memorized motor program, the delta-action chunk is largely predictable WITHOUT reading the object location — proprio + a position-conditioned prior is a near-sufficient statistic. Every prior H tried to inject grounding into a *representation* the L1 loss never forced the head to use. This is why they all nulled.

I have what I need to write the decisive shortlist.

---

# Ranked shortlist: attacking LIBERO-PRO position-swap collapse

**Root-cause confirmation from the code (load-bearing for everything below).** The action head (`MLPResNetBlock_Pro`, `action_heads.py`) receives three conditioning streams: `h_a` (action hidden states), `p = proprio_projector(proprio)` (the *current eef pose*, fed in directly), and `h_t` (the `num_patches` vision/task tokens). The only training signal is `L1(predicted_actions, ground_truth_actions)` on the normalized 8×7 delta-action chunk. **Given the start eef pose (proprio) plus a per-task position prior, the delta-action chunk is almost fully predictable without ever localizing the named object** — so position/proprio is a *sufficient statistic* for L1, and grounding is dead weight. Every failed H (H1 gate, H2a/b grasp head, H2c modulation) injected grounding into a *representation* while leaving this sufficient statistic intact. **The thing all failures missed is that the action head is never forced to depend on a grounding variable because proprio already explains the target.** The fixes below all share one principle: *make the L1-optimal solution require reading the named object's current location by destroying proprio/position as a sufficient statistic.*

---

## #1 — H4-PROP: Proprio-target ablation + relative-action reparametrization ("blind the shortcut, force the eyes")
**Effort: S.** **RUN THIS FIRST.**

**Mechanism.** The shortcut is *fed for free* via `p = proprio_projector(proprio)`. Two minimal, composable interventions, both warm-startable in ~2500 steps:
1. **Proprio dropout / orientation-only proprio.** During warm-start fine-tune, zero (or heavily dropout, p≈0.5) the **translational** components of proprio (`proprio[:, 0:3]`, eef xyz) before `proprio_projector`, keeping gripper + orientation. This removes the policy's free knowledge of *where the hand currently is in world frame*, which is what lets it co-memorize "object is always at canonical p → emit this motor program." With xyz proprio gone, the only remaining source of "where to go" is the **vision/task tokens `h_t`** — i.e. it must read the patch where the object actually is.
2. **(Optional, stronger) Object-relative action frame.** Re-express the supervised action chunk relative to a *visually anchored* origin instead of world frame. We already have `grasp_target` (free eef-xyz at grasp). Subtract a coarse, vision-derivable anchor so the regression target becomes "displacement from current eef toward the visible object," not "absolute canonical motor program." This is exactly the structure that transfers under swap.

**Supervision.** Existing demo actions, unchanged (intervention 1 is a pure *input ablation*, zero new labels). Intervention 2 reuses `grasp_target` (already computed, free, real-transferable).

**Why it reaches the action (the thing all failures missed).** This is the *only* hypothesis that attacks the **sufficient statistic itself** rather than adding a competing signal. H1/H2 added grounding *next to* proprio; the head ignored it because proprio still minimized L1. Remove the xyz proprio and L1 *cannot* be minimized without the vision tokens carrying object location — the gradient is forced through `h_t`. No new module needs to "win attention"; you delete the thing it was attending to.

**Novelty vs prior art.** Distinct from all named prior art (ReconVLA/ObjectVLA/RoboPoint/LIBERO-Plus) and from H1/H2/H3 here. Proprio-as-shortcut is a known *diagnosis* in the field but ablating translational proprio + relative-action reparametrization as a *position-debiasing fine-tune* for a small VLA is not a published method. Honest lineage: causal-confusion / "state shortcut" in imitation learning (de Haan et al.) — but applied here as a swap-collapse fix.

**Data-free / real-transferable.** Yes / yes. Proprio xyz and gripper exist on every real robot; relative-action reframing is standard and transfers.

**Single most important risk.** Removing xyz proprio may **hurt clean accuracy** (the policy genuinely uses eef xyz for closed-loop precision near contact). Mitigation: dropout (not hard-zero) so it keeps xyz when available but cannot *rely* on it; and the relative-action frame restores the needed geometry in a position-invariant form. If clean tanks below ~0.85, back off the dropout rate.

---

## #2 — H4-CONTRA: Same-image / different-instruction action contradiction (the honest, executable version of H3c)
**Effort: M.**

**Mechanism.** Manufacture the one training condition no prior H had: *identical pixels, different instruction → different action*, using only real frames + detector boxes — but fix H3c's fatal "synthesized off-manifold reach trajectory" flaw. **Do not relabel the full action chunk.** Instead:
- Run an open-vocab detector (OWLv2/GroundingDINO, real-RGB) offline on training frames to get boxes for *all* objects in scene.
- For a fraction p of batches, keep image A fixed, swap the instruction to name a *different* present object B, and supervise **only a short, geometrically-valid "orient/approach-direction" sub-signal** toward B — not a fabricated grasp. Concretely: supervise the *first-step translation-delta direction* (a unit-ish vector in eef frame) to point from current eef toward B's box anchor (image-plane direction is sufficient and detector-derivable), and **mask the rest of the chunk** (gripper, fine motion, later steps) from the loss for synthetic pairs.

So real pairs train the full chunk normally; synthetic contradiction pairs train *only the direction-of-first-motion* to be instruction-dependent. This makes "which way do I start moving" a function of language+detected-location, killing the vision-only positional shortcut, without ever fabricating a full unexecutable trajectory.

**Supervision.** First-step motion *direction* = (detected box anchor − current eef projection). Detector boxes are real-transferable; the direction signal is task-agnostic. No full-trajectory synthesis, no IK, no 2D→3D un-projection of a precise grasp point (only a *direction*, which is robust to depth ambiguity).

**Why it reaches the action.** It writes the contradiction directly into the **L1 target** of the action head's first step. The head physically cannot satisfy both (image A, "grab B") → start-left and (image A, "grab C") → start-right by reading position alone; it must read the instruction. This is the gradient that H1/H2 never produced.

**Novelty.** This is the sharpened, feasibility-repaired form flagged in the H3c verdict (direction-only, masked, no synthesized grasp). Distinct from LIBERO-Plus (new real trajectories), ObjectVLA (VL pairs), RoboPoint (sim keypoints + planner). Novel as a *direction-only, detector-distilled instruction-contradiction* fine-tune.

**Data-free / real-transferable.** Yes / yes (detector + proprio direction).

**Single most important risk.** Restricting supervision to first-step direction may be **too weak to flip a converged policy** — if only step-0 direction is instruction-dependent and the rest is position-memorized, the policy may emit the right initial nudge then revert to the memorized trajectory and still grasp the wrong (memorized-location) object. Mitigation: extend the direction supervision over the first K approach steps (still geometrically valid, still detector-derivable), not just step 0.

---

## #3 — H4-SPOT-DECOR: Detector spotlight in pixels, warm-started with *decorrelated* placement (the salvageable core of H3-TTVP)
**Effort: M.**

**Mechanism.** Paint a detector-grounded saliency spotlight at the named object's pixels into the RGB the policy actually consumes (`prepare_images_for_vla`), as in H3-TTVP — **but the make-or-break design choice the verdict identified is mandatory: at warm-start, decorrelate the spotlight from the memorized grasp location.** On training frames (which are never swapped), randomly place the spotlight on a *decoy detected object* on a fraction of frames and supervise the action's approach direction (as in H4-CONTRA) to **follow the spotlight**, not the memorized location. This teaches "go where the spotlight is," a load-bearing causal dependence. At eval, the detector puts the spotlight on the swapped object → policy follows it.

**Supervision.** Demo actions for correlated frames; spotlight-following direction (proprio/detector-derived) for decoy frames. Real-transferable.

**Why it reaches the action.** Intervenes on **raw input pixels / patch tokens** — the exact channel the Vision→Action policy demonstrably reads — and the decoy decorrelation forces the action to *track* the spotlight rather than treat it as redundant (which is precisely what collapsed H2 and would collapse naive TTVP).

**Novelty.** Detector-grounded pixel spotlight + decoy-decorrelated follow-supervision for a small action VLA is novel vs the named prior art; lineage to Red-Circle/FGVP visual prompting (CLIP/VLMs), but not as a position-memorization fix for action heads.

**Data-free / real-transferable.** Yes / yes (detector on real RGB).

**Single most important risk.** The info-bottleneck may **suppress the overlay as corruption** (StableVLA's whole design filters visual noise), and the zero-train regime will likely fail + may dent clean. This is why it ranks below #1/#2: it fights the architecture's own IB. Must be warm-started, never zero-shot.

---

## #4 — H4-MASKAUG: Patch-dropout of the canonical-location region (force re-localization by occlusion)
**Effort: S–M.**

**Mechanism.** A pure augmentation. During warm-start, with probability p, **drop/mask the vision patches at the object's canonical (memorized) location** (detector box on training RGB) while keeping the object visible elsewhere — OR, more simply, apply aggressive random *spatial* patch dropout / cutout to the agentview so the object is *not reliably at the same patch index*. The policy can no longer rely on "patch index k always contains the target." Combined with the unchanged action loss, it must learn to find the object wherever its patches are.

**Supervision.** None new (augmentation only). Real-transferable trivially.

**Why it reaches the action.** Breaks the fixed *patch-index → action* memorization by making the object's spatial token position unreliable, forcing content-based (not position-based) reading. Cheapest possible test of the "position-as-patch-index shortcut" theory.

**Novelty.** Cutout/patch-dropout is standard augmentation; *targeting it at the memorized object location to break positional binding in a VLA* is a mild but defensible twist. Lowest novelty here — value is as a cheap ablation/control.

**Data-free / real-transferable.** Yes / yes.

**Single most important risk.** Generic patch dropout may just teach **robustness to occlusion without inducing language-grounding** — the object could still be memorized at the *next* most-likely position. Best used as a control alongside #1, not a standalone fix.

---

## Which one to run FIRST and why

**Run #1 (H4-PROP: translational-proprio dropout) first.** Decisive reasons:
- It is **S effort** — a few-line change at the `proprio_projector` input plus a config flag; no detector, no new module, no offline preprocessing. Fastest possible falsification.
- It is the **only hypothesis that attacks the confirmed sufficient statistic directly.** The code proves `proprio` (eef xyz) is fed straight into the action head and that L1 on delta-actions is the sole signal. If, as the diagnosis says, "position is a sufficient statistic," then **xyz proprio is the cleanest place that statistic enters the action head.** Removing it is the highest-leverage, lowest-cost intervention, and it produces an unambiguous result: if swap moves, the proprio shortcut was load-bearing (huge finding); if swap stays 0.00 *and clean stays high*, you've **proven the shortcut lives in the vision patch-index prior, not proprio**, which immediately routes you to #3/#4 (pixel/patch interventions) with the search space cut in half. Either outcome is informative in ~28 min.
- It composes cleanly with #2 (contradiction pairs) as the next step: blind the proprio shortcut *and* inject the instruction contradiction.

Concretely: add a `proprio_xyz_dropout` flag in `finetune_fused.py`, zero/dropout `batch["proprio"][:, 0:3]` before the `vla(...)` call and before `action_head.predict_action`, warm-start from the released checkpoint 2500 steps on `libero_object_no_noops`, eval clean + swap + task. Keep the proprio-on baseline run for attribution.

---

## Is H2c (in-model action-conditioning, currently training) likely the answer?

**No — predict it nulls on swap, for a concrete, code-visible reason.** H2c's `TargetLocalizer` adds `mod = target_to_query(attended)` to the action_queries, but `target_to_query` is **zero-initialized** (confirmed in `target_localizer.py`). The *only* gradient pushing `mod` away from zero comes from the L1 action loss flowing back through the action_queries. But **proprio + the position prior already minimize L1** (the sufficient-statistic problem), so there is *no loss pressure* to grow `mod` — the path of least resistance is to keep `mod ≈ 0` and decode from proprio. The auxiliary `grasp_loss` trains the *localizer's prediction head*, exactly as in H2a where "the head learns but the action ignores it." H2c only changes *where* the discarded vector is injected (action_queries vs an external head), not *whether the action is forced to use it*. Same failure class. The architecture confirms it: as long as proprio is fed and L1 is the only action signal, a zero-init additive modulation has no incentive to activate.

**What to do when H2c also nulls (treat as expected, not a surprise):**
1. **Confirm the mechanism, don't just read SR.** Log the L2 norm / `tanh(gating)` of the realized `mod` and the localizer's grasp-prediction accuracy. The predicted signature is: grasp-pred accuracy good, `‖mod‖ ≈ 0`, swap = 0.00. That telemetry *proves* the diagnosis (action ignores grounding because proprio suffices) and justifies pivoting to #1.
2. **Immediately launch #1 (H4-PROP).** It directly tests whether removing proprio's xyz forces `mod`/vision to become load-bearing. Ideally run **H2c-localizer + proprio-xyz-dropout together**: now the localized target is the *only* remaining source of "where," so the zero-init modulation finally has a gradient reason to activate. This is the natural, code-minimal next experiment and reuses H2c's already-written `TargetLocalizer`.
3. If even *that* nulls, the shortcut is in the vision patch-index prior → go to **#3 (decorrelated spotlight)** / **#4 (location patch-dropout)**, which intervene in pixel/patch space rather than proprio.

**One-line summary:** every failure injected grounding *beside* a sufficient statistic the action head was free to ignore; the next move is to **delete the sufficient statistic (proprio xyz) and/or write the instruction-dependence into the L1 action target itself** — start with the S-effort proprio ablation (#1) because it is the cheapest, most decisive test of the confirmed root cause, and run it *combined with the existing H2c localizer* once H2c nulls.

Relevant files: `/mnt/data/sftp/data/quangpt3/gib-vla/StableVLA/prismatic/models/action_heads.py` (proprio fed as `p` into `MLPResNetBlock_Pro`), `/mnt/data/sftp/data/quangpt3/gib-vla/StableVLA/vla-scripts/finetune_fused.py` (forward pass, L1 loss, proprio injection at lines ~365/436), `/mnt/data/sftp/data/quangpt3/gib-vla/StableVLA/prismatic/vla/datasets/rlds/dataset.py:175-182` (free `grasp_target`), `/mnt/data/sftp/data/quangpt3/gib-vla/StableVLA/prismatic/util/target_localizer.py` (H2c zero-init modulation — the reason it will null).
